title: The boy who waters the plants
summary: Khoa is nine. Every morning before the other children come, he waters twenty plants in his school garden — alone, and on time.
body:
There is a small school in Hai Phong. In the school, there is a garden. In the garden, there are twenty plants.

A boy named Khoa waters the plants every morning. He is nine years old. He started this work two years ago, when he was seven. The teacher asked who wanted to do this small job. Khoa was the only one to put up his hand.

Now he comes at seven in the morning. The other children come at seven thirty. So Khoa has thirty minutes alone with the plants. He uses a small green watering can. He talks to the plants. He does not say much, but he says, 'Good morning,' to each one.

The plants are not the same. Some are tall. Some are short. Two are flowers. Eighteen are vegetables. The vegetables go to the school kitchen. The flowers go on the headmaster's desk.

The headmaster gave Khoa a small badge last month. The badge says, 'The Gardener.' Khoa wears it on his shirt. The other children laugh, a little. But they also wait to see what he does next.

Khoa is also a quiet boy at lessons. He does not raise his hand often. But in the garden, before seven thirty, he is something different. He is busy. He has a job. He matters.

I asked him, 'Why do you do this?'

He said, 'The plants are quiet too.'

grammarNotes.title: Past simple for finished actions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể các sự việc đã hoàn thành. Trong bài: 'He started this work two years ago.' / 'The teacher asked who wanted to do this small job.' / 'The headmaster gave Khoa a small badge last month.' Động từ đổi sang dạng quá khứ: start → started, ask → asked, give → gave.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the past simple for completed past actions. From the story: 'He started this work two years ago.' / 'The teacher asked who wanted to do this small job.' / 'The headmaster gave Khoa a small badge last month.' Verbs change to past form: start → started, ask → asked, give → gave.

cloze.template:

A boy named {{b2}} waters the plants every morning. He is {{b3}} years old. He started this work two years ago, when he was {{b4}}. The teacher asked who wanted to do this small job. Khoa was the only one to put up his hand.

Now he comes at seven in the {{b5}}. The other children come at seven thirty. So Khoa has thirty minutes alone with the plants. He uses a small {{b6}} watering can. He talks to the plants. He does not say much, but he says, 'Good morning,' to each one.

The plants are not the same. Some are tall. Some are short. Two are flowers. Eighteen are {{b7}}. The vegetables go to the school kitchen. The flowers go on the {{b8}}'s desk.

The headmaster gave Khoa a small {{b9}} last month. The badge says, 'The Gardener.' Khoa wears it on his shirt. The other children laugh, a little. But they also wait to see what he does next.

Khoa is also a {{b10}} boy at lessons. He does not raise his hand often. But in the garden, before seven thirty, he is something different. He is busy. He has a {{b11}}. He matters.

I asked him, '{{b12}} do you do this?'

He said, 'The plants are quiet too.'

blanks.explanation: He started 'when he was seven' — two years before age nine.
A small green watering can — the colour given in the story.
Eighteen of the plants are vegetables.
The flowers go on the headmaster's desk.
The headmaster gave Khoa a small badge that says 'The Gardener'.
'Khoa is also a quiet boy at lessons.'
'Why do you do this?' — the writer's final question.

translationVi:
Có một trường học nhỏ ở Hải Phòng. Trong trường có một khu vườn. Trong vườn có hai mươi cây.

Một cậu bé tên là Khoa tưới cây mỗi sáng. Em chín tuổi. Em bắt đầu công việc này hai năm trước, khi em bảy tuổi. Cô giáo hỏi ai muốn làm công việc nhỏ này. Khoa là người duy nhất giơ tay.

Giờ em đến lúc bảy giờ sáng. Các bạn khác đến lúc bảy rưỡi. Vậy là Khoa có ba mươi phút một mình với cây. Em dùng một cái bình tưới nhỏ màu xanh. Em nói chuyện với cây. Em không nói nhiều, nhưng em chào từng cây một câu 'Chào buổi sáng'.

Các cây không giống nhau. Có cây cao. Có cây thấp. Hai cây là hoa. Mười tám cây là rau. Rau dùng cho bếp ăn của trường. Hoa thì đặt trên bàn của thầy hiệu trưởng.

Tháng trước thầy hiệu trưởng tặng Khoa một huy hiệu nhỏ. Huy hiệu ghi: 'Người làm vườn.' Khoa đeo nó trên áo. Các bạn khác cười, một chút. Nhưng các bạn cũng chờ xem em sẽ làm gì tiếp theo.

Trong lớp, Khoa cũng là một cậu bé trầm tính. Em ít khi giơ tay. Nhưng ở trong vườn, trước bảy rưỡi, em là một con người khác. Em bận. Em có việc. Em có ý nghĩa.

Tôi hỏi em: 'Vì sao em làm việc này?'

Em nói: 'Cây cối cũng trầm tính ạ.'

questions.prompt: How many plants are in the school garden?
questions.hint: Look at the very first paragraph.

questions.prompt: How old was Khoa when he started this job?
Who chose to do this job, when the teacher first asked?
options: Khoa was the only one to put up his hand
questions.explanation: 'Khoa was the only one to put up his hand.'

questions.prompt: What time does Khoa come to school, and what time do the other children come?
options: He comes at six; others at seven
He comes at seven; others at seven thirty
He comes at eight; others at nine
questions.explanation: 'He comes at seven in the morning. The other children come at seven thirty.'

questions.prompt: What does Khoa say to the plants?
What happens to the flowers Khoa grows?
options: They go on the headmaster's desk
questions.explanation: 'The flowers go on the headmaster's desk.'

questions.prompt: What did the headmaster give Khoa last month?
options: A small badge that says 'The Gardener'
questions.explanation: 'The headmaster gave Khoa a small badge last month. The badge says, "The Gardener."'

questions.prompt: Why does Khoa do this job, in his own words?
questions.hint: Look at the very last line.

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the story?
options: A quiet boy has found one place where he matters
questions.explanation: 'But in the garden, before seven thirty, he is something different. He is busy. He has a job. He matters.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Khoa says the plants 'are quiet too'. Think of one small task you do that no one asks you to do. What does it give you that the louder parts of your day cannot?
